chapters.title: Keyumars — The First King
chapters.summary: From the mountains of Pars, Keyumars rules a world still soft with dawn. He clothes himself in leopard skin and the beasts bow.
chapters.story: From the mountains of Pars, Keyumars ruled a world still soft with dawn. He clothed himself in leopard skin, and the beasts of the field came down to bow at his feet. His son Siyamak fell to the demon Khazura, and Keyumars wept thirty days for the loss. The first kingdom was forged in mourning and song.

chapters.title: Hushang — The Spark of Fire
chapters.summary: Hushang strikes flint at a serpent and instead wakes the eternal flame. Sade, the festival of fire, is born.
chapters.story: Hushang the wise grandson struck flint at a serpent of black scales. The stone missed, but a spark leapt up and a tree caught fire. From that night the people knew flame, and Hushang named the festival Sade. Iron was forged. Water was led to fields. The first crafts were taught to humankind.

chapters.title: Tahmuras — Binder of Demons
chapters.summary: Tahmuras the Div-band rides through smoke and chains the demons. They beg for life and trade their secret: writing.
chapters.story: Tahmuras the Div-band rode through smoke and shadow with a club of cypress. He chained the demons and dragged them to the foot of his throne. To save their lives the demons taught him thirty alphabets — Greek, Tazi, Pahlavi, Sogdian. Knowledge was bought with mercy, and the world remembered.

chapters.title: Jamshid — The Golden Throne
chapters.summary: Jamshid divides the people into four crafts and sits a kingdom of crystal. Pride finally outshines the sun.
chapters.story: Jamshid divided the people into four crafts: priests, warriors, farmers, artisans. He raised a crystal throne and was carried into the sky by demons on Nowruz. Three hundred years of light. Then pride: he claimed to be a god, and the divine grace, the farr, departed from him.

chapters.title: Zahhak — The Serpent King
chapters.summary: Two serpents grow from Zahhak's shoulders. Their daily price is two human brains. A thousand years of darkness.
chapters.story: From the kiss of Iblis, two black serpents sprang from Zahhak's shoulders. Each day demanded two human brains, drawn from the youth of Pars. A thousand years of darkness pressed on the land — until a blacksmith's apron rose as a banner.

chapters.title: Fereydun — The Liberator
chapters.summary: Kaveh the blacksmith raises his apron as a banner. Fereydun's bull-headed mace ends a tyrant.
chapters.story: Kaveh the blacksmith raised his leather apron on a spear, and the people of Pars marched. Fereydun took up the bull-headed mace forged for this hour. Zahhak was bound to Damavand's mountain forever. The kingdom split among Fereydun's three sons: Iraj, Tur, Salm — and from their feud, the Shahnameh's wars began.

chapters.title: Manuchehr — The Avenger
chapters.summary: From Fereydun's sorrow, a new king rises. Manuchehr avenges Iraj — and a world still shaped by blood begins again.

chapters.title: Nozar — The Kingdom Divides
chapters.summary: Nozar inherits the throne but not the virtue. The age of great kings dims toward its first dusk.

chapters.title: Zal — The Albino Prince
chapters.summary: Born white-haired, cast to the mountain, raised by Simorgh, Zal returns to Pars on wings of myth.
chapters.story: Born white-haired, the prince Zal was cast onto the mountain by his father Sam, who feared the omen. The Simorgh found him and raised him with her chicks for years. When Sam repented and climbed the mountain, the Simorgh gave Zal back, and one feather, to be burned in greatest need.

chapters.title: Rudabeh — The Tower of Hair
chapters.summary: A princess lets her hair down from the tower. Love enters the chronicle before war — and Rostam's father is won.

chapters.title: Birth of Rostam
chapters.summary: Zal burns the Simorgh's feather. Through pain and fire, the greatest pahlavan enters the world.

chapters.title: Rostam — Pahlavan of the Age
chapters.summary: Seven labours, one Rakhsh, no equal under the sky. Rostam becomes the world's pillar of arms.
chapters.story: Seven labours: a lion in the night, a desert without water, a dragon, a witch, a div-king Akvan, the demon king of Mazandaran. Rakhsh chose his rider himself. From that day Rostam was Iran's pillar of arms — and his shadow stretched over five generations of kings.

chapters.title: Sohrab — Son of the Storm
chapters.summary: Two armies camp on opposite shores. Father and son, unknowing, raise blades against each other.
chapters.story: From Tahmineh's chamber, Sohrab grew tall as a cedar. He led the army of Turan into Iran, searching for the father he had never seen. Father and son met on the battlefield, neither speaking their name. Rostam's blade fell. The bracelet on Sohrab's arm answered every question too late.

chapters.title: Siavash — The Pure Prince
chapters.summary: A prince too pure for a corrupt court walks through fire to prove his innocence. He will not return.

chapters.title: Kay Kavus — The Reckless King
chapters.summary: A king who challenges the sky and loses. Rostam must rescue him — again and again.

chapters.title: Kay Khosrow — The Chosen King
chapters.summary: Son of the martyred Siavash, Kay Khosrow is destiny's chosen king. His reign is the chronicle's golden age.

chapters.title: Akvan Div — The Demon of the Lake
chapters.summary: A demon that grants wishes by twisting them. Rostam is hurled into the sea — and emerges unconquered.

chapters.title: Bijan and Manijeh — Pit and Rose
chapters.title_fa: بیژن و منیژه — چاه و گل
chapters.summary: A warrior falls into a pit for love. A princess bribes the world to free him. A rescue across three winters.

chapters.title: The Great War — Iran and Turan
chapters.summary: The final war between Iran and Turan. Kay Khosrow crosses the Oxus. A thousand years of enmity closes.

chapters.title: Lohrasp — The Humble King
chapters.summary: Kay Khosrow vanishes into the mountain and passes the crown to Lohrasp — a transfer that surprises a kingdom.

chapters.title: Goshtasp and Zoroaster
chapters.summary: Zoroaster comes to the court of Goshtasp. Fire temples are lit. A new faith is threaded into the old chronicle.

chapters.title: Esfandiyar — The Brazen-Bodied
chapters.summary: Steel cannot cut him, except where his eyes can be reached.
chapters.story: Esfandiyar was bathed in fire by the prophet Zoroaster, and his body became brazen. No iron could harm him — except his eyes, kept shut at the moment of immersion. His seven labours rivalled Rostam's. His ending arrived in a hail of feathers and arrows under the cypress.

chapters.title: Esfandiyar's Seven Labours
chapters.summary: Before facing Rostam, Esfandiyar's seven tests begin. Each labour is a world in itself.

chapters.title: The Great Clash
chapters.summary: Two pahlavans who cannot both live. One by duty, one by pride. The Simorgh's feather decides everything.

chapters.title: Simorgh — The Phoenix Council
chapters.summary: Wings the colour of the sun. The mountain remembers a wisdom older than language.
chapters.story: Wings the colour of the sun. The Simorgh's nest holds wisdom older than the first speech. She watches the line of Zal across generations, and burns the feather only when the world's last hope hangs by a thread.

chapters.title: Rostam's Last Stand
chapters.summary: A half-brother's treachery. A pit of iron stakes. The world's pillar of arms falls — and the chronicle goes quiet.

chapters.title: Bahman — Avenger's Son
chapters.summary: Esfandiyar's son rules with grief and ambition. The Kayanian house turns on itself.

chapters.title: Homay — The Warrior Queen
chapters.summary: A queen, not a king, takes the throne — and proves sharper than the swords around her.

chapters.title: Darab — The Foundling Prince
chapters.summary: Abandoned in a chest on the river, raised by a launderer, Darab reclaims a kingdom that forgot him.

chapters.title: Dara — The Last Achaemenid
chapters.summary: The last Achaemenid. Pride before the Greek tide. A king who would not bend, and so is broken.

chapters.title: Alexander — The Two-Horned
chapters.summary: Ferdowsi's Alexander carries both philosophy and a sword. He is Iranian by a twist of blood — and the world bows.

chapters.title: The Ashkanian Age
chapters.summary: The Arsacids rule in fragments. Many small kings, many small wars. The chronicle dims between golden ages.

chapters.title: Ardavan — The Last Arsacid
chapters.summary: The last Parthian king sees a new fire rising from Pars. He fights it — and the old age ends.

chapters.title: Ardeshir I — Founder of Sassan
chapters.summary: A herdsman's ambition, a court astrologer's prophecy, and a dynasty is born. Ardeshir refounds Pars.

chapters.title: Shapur the Great — Rome Kneels
chapters.summary: Two Roman armies captured. Shapur's name is carved in rock for eternity.

chapters.title: Bahram Gur — The Lion Hunter
chapters.summary: The lion hunter. The poet-king who rhymes his own legend. Bahram vanishes into the earth with his horse.

chapters.title: Yazdegerd the Sinner
chapters.summary: The king who earned the worst name in the chronicle. Cruelty breeds only its mirror.

chapters.title: Bahram Chubin — The Rebel
chapters.summary: The greatest general who dared to call himself king. His rebellion reshapes the Sassanian world.

chapters.title: Khosrow Anushirvan — The Just
chapters.summary: Courts poets, reforms the land, defeats the Hephthalites. The golden height of the Sassanians.

chapters.title: Nushzad — The Rebellious Prince
chapters.summary: The Christian son of the Just King rebels against his father — and is answered with iron.

chapters.title: Hormuz — The Crumbling Crown
chapters.summary: The son who squanders the father's legacy. The throne becomes the prize in a game of knives.

chapters.title: Khosrow Parviz — The Conqueror
chapters.summary: The conqueror of Jerusalem, collector of horses and marvels. His pride dims even the sun.

chapters.title: Shirin — Queen of Love
chapters.summary: Queen, lover, builder. Shirin's courage outlasts the empires around her.

chapters.title: The Crumbling Crown
chapters.summary: Fourteen rulers in thirty years. Civil war, betrayal, and plague. The Sassanian house devours itself.

chapters.title: Yazdegerd III — The Last King
chapters.summary: The last Sassanian king runs east with his treasury and no army. The chronicle watches him and weeps.

chapters.title: The Arab Conquest — Fire and Sword
chapters.summary: Qadisiyya and Nihavand. Two battles, two seasons, and a thousand years of Persian kingship ends.

chapters.title: The Mourning of Pars
chapters.summary: Ferdowsi names the grief. The fire temples are dark. But the language of Pars keeps burning in every verse.

chapters.title: Memory Over Sword
chapters.summary: What the sword cannot keep, words can. Ferdowsi's testament to the power of Persian memory.

chapters.title: Ferdowsi's Legacy
chapters.summary: Thirty years. Sixty thousand verses. A tomb in Tus. The Shahnameh outlives every king it describes.

chapters.title: The Final War — Ages End
chapters.summary: The chronicle closes where it began: with fire, with iron, with kings.
chapters.story: The chronicle closes where it began: with fire, with iron, with kings. Ferdowsi's verses outlive the empires they describe. What was once a kingdom of crystal becomes a kingdom of memory, and every reader joins the lineage of Pars.
